_format_doc: Same inline-annotation format as training_data.json: '[value](type)'. Held out from training; only used to measure generalization (val_loss, intent_F1, slot_token_accuracy) per epoch.
_provenance_notes: (a) ~25 entries are adapted from SNIPS NLU benchmark (PlayMusic intent) — real crowdsourced human queries collected by Snips/Sonos, Apache-2.0 licensed: https://github.com/sonos/nlu-benchmark/tree/master/2017-06-custom-intent-engines/PlayMusic . Slot mapping: SNIPS 'track'→our 'song'; SNIPS 'sort=best/top'→our 'rating'; SNIPS 'service' dropped (not a tool we expose); SNIPS 'music_item' dropped (we don't distinguish song/symphony/melody).

(b) ~55 entries are hand-written, deliberately using artists/songs/lyric topics/descriptions that DO NOT appear in generate_data.py's vocabulary. Purpose: detect memorization. If validation F1 stays high here, the model is genuinely generalizing rather than pattern-matching the generator's vocab.
(c) A few entries use casual phrasings (lowercase, 'plz', abbreviations, sentence fragments) that real users type but the templates don't cover.

Imported SNIPS NLU benchmark / PlayMusic intent (Apache-2.0). Slot map: track->song, sort->rating, artist/album/genre/year preserved; service/music_item/playlist dropped. (351 examples added to validation set, seed=2026).

Imported CPCD (Google Research, CC-BY 4.0) joined with Doh et al. ISMIR'24 intent annotations (seungheondoh/cpcd-intent). Intent labels from Doh; artist/song spans matched against CPCD's per-dialog track metadata; genre/mood/tempo/energy/year/time_range tagged via domain vocabulary substring match. (181 examples added to validation set, seed=2026).

Imported user_curated_v1: real-user-style queries covering gaps in synthetic+SNIPS+CPCD — dash song-similarity, multi-artist, top-songs-of-X, numeric BPM tempo, hyphenated tempo descriptors, vocal-style words, short multi-attribute descriptions. (16 examples added to validation set, seed=4242).
Augmented from existing training data with carrier-phrase, synonym, word-order, and casing/punctuation paraphrases. (223 examples added to validation set, seed=7777).
